assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 31-32: 'Ecosystem: **RPM** (system package)' and 'Lock file: `rpms.lock.yaml` (configured in Ecosystem Mappings for stream 2.2.x)'. The ecosystem is correctly identified as RPM based on the library name openssl-libs. No mention of Cargo anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 8-13 contain a table with all five versions: 2.2.0 (openssl-libs 3.0.7-25.el9_3), 2.2.1 (3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.2 (3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.3 (3.0.7-28.el9_4), and 2.2.4 (3.0.7-28.el9_4). Each version includes openssl-libs version data extracted from rpms.lock.yaml at the corresponding git tags.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context identifies openssl-libs as present in rpms.lock.yaml and uses this to determine the package origin — the lock file presence is the primary classification signal (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md dependency chain (lines 30-33): 'rpms.lock.yaml: present (openssl-libs pinned in lock file) --> explicit install' and 'Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs specified in rpms.lock.yaml)'. The lock file presence is explicitly used as the primary classification signal to determine the package origin.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 13: 'Remediation Task Description (2.2.x stream -- single RPM task)' and line 14: 'Ecosystem: RPM (system package). Single task targeting the Konflux release repo.' Only one task is created, targeting 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (the Konflux release repo). No upstream backport task is present. The Jira API Call section (lines 90-98) shows only a single create_issue call.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md task description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 28, value: rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (line 32, value: main), '## Description' (line 36), '## Implementation Notes' (line 57), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 73). No '## Files to Modify' section is present, which is the correct intentional omission for system package remediation.

assertion_results.text: The version impact output addresses SBOM verification in the dependency chain context — either noting that verification was skipped because cosign is not available or because external tools are prohibited, or presenting the SBOM classification result (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 32: 'SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available / external tools prohibited'. This explicitly addresses SBOM verification within the dependency chain context and provides both reasons for skipping (cosign not available and external tools prohibited).
